# Requirement: FR-007

- Title: Prevent scrollbar-induced layout shift between top-level pages
- Status: Active

## Requirement Statement

The system shall keep the overall page frame visually stable when users move between short and long top-level pages, so changing scrollbar presence does not cause noticeable horizontal layout shift.

## Rationale

When one page shows a vertical scrollbar and another does not, the content can appear to jump sideways during navigation. This feels glitchy and reduces the perceived polish of the site.

## Acceptance Conditions

- Home, Projects, and Contact keep a consistent content position when switching between routes with different content heights.
- The solution is implemented in the shared layout rather than repeated per page.
- Scroll behavior remains usable and accessible on desktop and mobile browsers.
- The fix does not introduce broken layout, clipping, or route regressions.

## Success Metrics

- Cross-page navigation no longer produces a visible horizontal shift caused by scrollbar appearance changes.
- Build, lint, and format checks remain green after the change.

## Non-Goals

- Custom scrollbar theming.
- JavaScript-driven scroll effects or animation-heavy transitions.

## Constraints

- Preserve the static-first architecture and simple shared-layout approach.
- Keep the implementation compatible with base-path-aware routing and existing responsive layout rules.
